title: Palicourea thornei
intro: Palicourea thornei, also known as the Thorne's palicourea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Palicourea thornei include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, such as palicourea, thorensine, and flavonoids like quercetin and kaempferol, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Palicourea thornei include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions in traditional medicine.
taxonomy: Palicourea thornei is classified within the plant family Rubiaceae. The genus Palicourea is a group of flowering plants native to the Americas. Within the Rubiaceae family, Palicourea thornei is further classified as a member of the tribe Palicoureeae. Palicourea thornei is a species of flowering plant endemic to the Neotropics. Its classification is still a subject of ongoing research and study in the field of botany.
